assertions.text: V1 was traveling east on a one-way exit ramp.
V2, a tractor-trailer, was parked on the paved shoulder of the exit ramp facing east.
V1 departed the roadway to the left before returning to the roadway.
After returning to the roadway, V1 departed the roadway to the right.
V1's front plane contacted the back plane of V2's trailer (V1 struck the rear of V2).
There was a single impact between V1 and V2 in which V1 contacted V2's trailer.
V2 was pushed forward by the impact and came to rest near the point of impact.
After the impact, V1 rotated clockwise.
Following rotation, V1 rebounded (bounced) back and came to rest on the left shoulder facing east.
The collision produced enough force to push V2 forward and cause V1 to rotate and bounce back (indicating a non-trivial collision, more than a light tap).

verdicts.evidence: V1 was traveling east is consistent with recorded forward speed: [first_value 98.0 km/h at t=-4.75 s](T2) and [last_value 96.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), indicating travel at highway speed prior to impact.

Presence, type and facing of V2 (tractor-trailer parked on shoulder facing east) are not recorded by V1 sensors; telemetry contains no explicit information about V2 location or orientation, so this is unverifiable.

Departure to the left before returning to roadway is a lateral path detail not resolvable: steering shows a peak signed deviation [33.0 deg at t=0.0 s](T2) but device-frame axes and GPS lateral position are not available, so leftward departure is unverifiable.

Departure to the right after returning to roadway is a lateral path detail; steering data alone ([peak raw 40.5 deg at t=0.0 s](T2)) cannot determine sequence of left then right departures without vehicle-frame position, so unverifiable.

Impact is frontal-to-rear (V1 struck rear of V2) is supported by the crash-pulse longitudinal delta-V being strongly negative: [max delta-V -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T2), which indicates a large deceleration consistent with V1's front contacting the back of another vehicle.

Telemetry shows a single dominant crash pulse: detection reports one delta-V peak [magnitude 98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3) and the cumulative delta-V final value is [ -98.2 km/h](T2), consistent with a single major impact event.
Whether V2 was pushed forward and came to rest near impact is about V2 motion; V1 telemetry does not record V2 displacement, so this is unverifiable.

Post-impact rotation direction of V1 cannot be determined from available telemetry: gyro/heading/net rotation metrics are not present (detect_events rotation thresholds did not report net rotation), so clockwise rotation is unverifiable.

Rebound and final resting position on left shoulder facing east are vehicle trajectory/endpose facts not present in V1's telemetry (no GPS track or final heading in provided channels), so unverifiable.

Severity claim is supported: crash-pulse shows a large cumulative longitudinal delta-V of [ -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s](T3) and EDR summary lists max_delta_v_long_kmh [ -99.1 km/h](T1), indicating a high-energy collision greater than a light tap.

result.rationale: Telemetry provides strong evidence of a single, large frontal deceleration event (large negative delta-V magnitude [ -98.6 km/h](T3) and EDR summary [ -99.1 km/h](T1)), supporting impact direction and severity claims; lateral maneuvers, V2 motion, and post-impact rotation/position are not observable from V1 sensor channels and are unverifiable.

citations.claim: peak raw 40.5 deg at t=0.0 s
max delta-V -98.6 km/h at t=0.24 s

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
